name: Mayjing Red Oil Wonton
why: 1976 3rd-gen icon in Dinghao basement, on 500 Bowls list, Mayor Chiang Wan-an's favorite.

name: Lao Deng 1949
why: 70+yr 5th-gen Sichuan dandan icon in Yongkang, praised by critic Hu Tian-lan.

name: Chuan Wei Mian Dian
why: Cult Sichuan noodle spot open only 4.5hrs/day; dandan and red-oil wonton; featured in Japanese media.

name: Tian Ran Tai Hunan
why: 1955 70-year Hunan institution, set meals from NT$130, famed chopped-chili fish head.

name: Momodarling Roujiamo
why: Iconic Yongkang Shaanxi roujiamo with fry-then-bake flaky bun (食尚玩家, WalkerLand).

name: Xiao Li Zi Lanzhou Noodles
zh: 小李子蘭州牛肉拉麵館
why: 20+yr Lanzhou hand-pulled beef noodle with medicinal broth; Beef Noodle Festival regular.

name: Uyghur Xinjiang BBQ
why: 10+yr celebrity-favored authentic Xinjiang charcoal cumin skewers (NT$80/2).

name: Wang Li Shi Zhajiangmian
why: Taipei's most-cited modern zhajiangmian: black-pork belly, 13 spices, near Tonghua Market.

name: Yi Pin Shanxi Knife-Cut Noodles
why: Robot knife-cut noodles since 1998; Tianmu's top noodle house (ETtoday, WalkerLand).

name: A-Liang Dumpling House
why: One of Nanjichang's three great dumpling shops; big hand-wrapped Dongbei dumplings.

name: Hsiung Chi Scallion Pancake
why: Gongguan's only Bib Gourmand stall; 30-yr flaky cong zhua bing from NT$40, build-your-own toppings.

name: Yundish Yunnan Rice Noodles
why: 30-year East District icon for crossing-the-bridge noodles and Yunnan thin-sliced pork.

name: Dianwei Kitchen
why: Old-brand cheap Yunnan (est. 1999): crossing-the-bridge noodles, jiaoma chicken.

name: Glybistro
why: Buzzy reservation-only mi-gan and fried pork skin from streamer 鳥屎; PTT/Dcard favorite.

name: Tim Ho Wan
why: World-famous HK dim sum brand (1-star in HK); baked char siu bao under NT$140.

name: Jixing 24h Dim Sum
why: Taipei's original 24h HK dim sum (since 1989), 13k+ reviews; carts NT$100-300.

name: Chung Chia Sheng Jian Bao
why: Bib Gourmand 6+ yrs at Shilin Night Market; pan-fried buns NT$18 each.

name: Jiahong Roast Goose
why: Zhongshan's cult HK siu mei; crispy roast goose/duck bentos NT$150, sell out fast.

name: Honolulu Cafe
why: HK's first-gen cha chaan teng (1940); famous 192-layer egg tart, pineapple bun, milk tea.

name: Bokey Tea Restaurant
why: East District's 20-yr retro HK cha chaan teng; yuanyang, French toast, HK noodles.

name: Dim Dim Sum
why: Famous HK dim sum chain; molten custard piggy bun, shrimp dumplings NT$78-138.

name: Kao Chi
why: Since 1949, predates Din Tai Fung; iron-griddle sheng jian bao, XLB.

name: Incredible Roast
why: Cult Cantonese siu mei; crispy-skin belly pork, char siu, roast duck sets (Eating in Taipei).
